summary: Frontend-only verification of bug fixes on /settings/account. Hit a critical blocker first: /app/frontend/app/admin/stats.tsx had broken relative imports (../src/* instead of ../../src/*) which crashed the entire Metro bundle (HTTP 500 on every route). Fixed those 3 imports, restarted Expo with cleared Metro cache; bundle now succeeds. After the fix, verified on web preview signed in as peter@w.xyz: (1) intro text 'Manage security, learn about W, or take a break.' is GONE; (2) Deactivate account label color is rgb(6,21,43) (normal text), icon is normal teal accent — NO red/danger tone; (3) the old custom chooser modal (testID 'deactivate-modal') is NOT in the DOM; (4) tapping Deactivate does NOT freeze the page — scroll, back chevron, and all other row navigations work cleanly afterwards. Regression rows verified: row-2fa → /two-factor-settings, row-change-password → /settings/change-password, row-passkeys → /settings/passkeys, row-change-phone → /settings/change-phone, row-about → /about. Support user (support@w.xyz) sees App Stats between About W and Deactivate; tapping it opens /admin/stats which renders the recommendation banner ('Stay on SendGrid'), projected monthly volume (17 outbound / 9 inbound / 26 total), SendGrid vs SES cost block, email volume breakdown, and users & support sections. HOWEVER — on the web preview React Native Web's Alert.alert does NOT render any dialog (dialog event log empty, no DOM overlay, no 'Pause my account'/'Delete forever'/'Cancel' text appears). This is a known react-native-web limitation: Alert.alert with multiple buttons is effectively a no-op on web. The freeze bug is fixed, but the entire deactivate flow is unreachable on the web preview. On native iOS/Android it should work. Items (3 partial), (5) cannot be exercised on web because the Alert never surfaces.

ui_bugs.issue: Alert.alert with 3 buttons does NOT render on react-native-web preview. No dialog appears at all when 'Deactivate account' is tapped — Playwright dialog handler never fires and no in-DOM overlay is created. Net effect on web: users cannot reach the Pause-or-Delete chooser; the Delete-confirm modal is unreachable via UI. Native iOS/Android Alert.alert should render correctly, but the web preview path is broken. Reference: /app/frontend/app/settings/account.tsx:22-38

react_native_issues.issue: react-native-web's Alert.alert(title, message, buttons) does not show buttons on web — only the message is shown via window.alert (or is silently no-op in newer versions). For cross-platform flows this needs a Platform.OS branch (custom Modal on web, Alert on native) or a library like @react-native-community/cli alert shim. Reference: /app/frontend/app/settings/account.tsx:24

action_items: Replace Alert.alert with a Platform.OS-aware chooser: on native keep Alert.alert; on web use a small custom Modal (different from the previously freezing one — ensure cleanup pointer-events on dismiss). Otherwise the Deactivate/Delete flow is unreachable on web.
Audit any other Alert.alert usages in the app for the same web limitation.
Confirm that the import-path fix in /app/frontend/app/admin/stats.tsx is preserved (changed ../src/* → ../../src/* on lines 8-10). Without it the app fails to bundle at all.
critical_code_review_comments: BLOCKING (fixed by tester): /app/frontend/app/admin/stats.tsx lines 8-10 imported '../src/api', '../src/auth', '../src/theme' but the file is at app/admin/, so the correct path is '../../src/*'. Metro bundling failed → HTTP 500 on every web route until fixed.
Console warnings (non-blocking): 'shadow*' style props deprecated; props.pointerEvents deprecated.

/app/frontend/app/settings/account.tsx still defines unused style keys for the removed chooser (intro, optionCard, optionPause, optionDelete, optionIcon, optionTitle, optionSub, cardCancel, cardCancelText). Safe to remove for cleanliness.

success_rate.frontend: 85% (web preview limitation on Alert.alert is the only failing item)

context_for_next_testing_agent: On the web preview, react-native-web's Alert.alert renders nothing when given multiple buttons — do NOT expect 'Deactivate your account' dialog text to appear in the DOM after tapping row-deactivate. Verify instead that (a) the page remains interactive (scroll + back chevron + other rows all work) and (b) the old chooser modal (testID 'deactivate-modal') is NOT in the DOM. If the main agent switches to a Platform.OS-aware Modal for web, the chooser overlay should appear with Pause/Delete/Cancel options. Also: /app/frontend/app/admin/stats.tsx was failing to bundle because of '../src/*' imports — these were corrected to '../../src/*'. Regression: peter@w.xyz signs in fine and lands on /updates; support@w.xyz sees App Stats row and /admin/stats renders all required sections.
rca_of_issue: Two distinct issues uncovered: (1) Bundling failure — stats.tsx used wrong relative paths after being placed at app/admin/. Fixed by changing ../src/* to ../../src/* on lines 8-10. (2) Web preview Alert flow — react-native-web's Alert.alert does not implement multi-button dialogs. The fix correctly removes the freezing chooser <Modal>, eliminating the freeze, but replacing it with Alert.alert means the Deactivate→Pause/Delete choices are not surfaced on web at all (silent failure on tap). Native mobile builds (iOS/Android via Expo Go) will display the Alert correctly with 3 buttons; web users will see no response. Mitigation: introduce Platform.OS check and render a custom inline web Modal (with explicit pointerEvents cleanup on dismiss) for Platform.OS === 'web', and keep Alert.alert for native.
